title: One Claude, Six Doors
voiceScript: I shipped better automations the day I stopped treating Claude like one app. My failure mode was forcing everything through web chat. Tasks stalled, context leaked, and I blamed the model instead of my workflow. It's not one app, it's one intelligence with six doors. Chat to think, Cowork for hands-off desktop work, Code to build, and the API to run unattended. The rule is simple. One intelligence, six doors, two questions: who does the work, and how much should it act alone. Choose the right interface for the right autonomy level. Pick the door, not the panic. Follow for more.

scenes.voiceText: I shipped better automations the day I stopped treating Claude like one app.
scenes.headline: Stop treating Claude like *one app*

scenes.voiceText: My failure mode was forcing everything through web chat. Tasks stalled, context leaked, and I blamed the model instead of my workflow.
scenes.headline: One door for *everything*

scenes.voiceText: It's not one app, it's one intelligence with six doors. Chat to think, Cowork for hands-off desktop work, Code to build, and the API to run unattended.
scenes.headline: One brain, *six* doors

scenes.voiceText: The rule is simple. One intelligence, six doors, two questions: who does the work, and how much should it act alone.
scenes.headline: The whole decision

scenes.voiceText: Choose the right interface for the right autonomy level.
scenes.headline: Right door, right *autonomy*

scenes.voiceText: Pick the door, not the panic. Follow for more.

source: LinkedIn post + guide section 2 'The Claude Universe: Every Way to Use It'. Core idea: one Claude intelligence reached through six doors - Claude.ai web chat (writing/analysis/brainstorm; Projects + Artifacts), Desktop app (daily home, hosts Cowork + Code), Mobile (quick Qs + remote approve), Cowork (autonomous agent that does file/spreadsheet/deck work on your desktop, no code), Claude Code (terminal/editor, builds + fixes software across files), API (developer plumbing, unattended automations). Pick the door by TWO questions: who does the work (you steering vs Claude acting) and how much autonomy (low = chat, high = Cowork/Code/API). Failure mode = forcing everything through web chat: tasks stall, context leaks, inconsistent outputs, blaming the model not the workflow. Right way = tasks start in chat for clarity, then graduate to Cowork or Code once scope is stable, API only when it must run unattended. Systems thinking: right interface for the right autonomy level. Post CTA: comment DOORS for the decision checklist.
